page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton2 processor using the 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz with no hyperthreading. The memory configuration consists of 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 24284.25 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8613.0. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 956.0 and a weak single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.34 GB/sec. Cryptographic and text processing tasks also show lower relative performance, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 2171.79. Memory bandwidth is strong for small cached blocks at 946.88 GB/sec but drops to 169.51 GB/sec for large uncached blocks.

The c6gn.4xlarge is designed for network-intensive and compute-bound workloads. It achieves average performance in static web serving at 19.19 GB/sec and database operations, making it suitable for web servers, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and software compilation. Its ARM-based architecture provides a cost-efficient alternative to x86 instances for workloads optimized for multi-core scaling, though it is poorly suited for LLM inference or single-threaded execution paths.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by the AWS Graviton2 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 24284.25 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8613.0, alongside average static web serving and database performance. However, single-core performance and cryptographic operations are relatively weak. The c6gn.4xlarge is best suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and network-intensive applications such as static web serving, offering a cost-efficient ARM-based profile for parallelizable tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 32 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for multi-threaded network workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated cores, 32 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM-based compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and high network bandwidth for parallelized workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton2 ARM64 physical cores at 2.5 GHz.
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers average multi-core performance for software compilation and database operations.
Exhibits lower single-core efficiency and weak cryptographic performance.
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and network-heavy tasks.
